task: Write a Python function title_words(s) returning the string with every whitespace-separated word title-cased (first letter upper, the rest lower) and runs of whitespace collapsed to single spaces, except that words already entirely uppercase stay exactly as they are. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles the edge case where an empty string is input.
trace:
** 
I first split the input string `s` into a list of words. Then, I iterate over each word in this list. If a word is already entirely uppercase, I append it to the result list without any changes. Otherwise, I title-case the word by capitalizing its first letter and making the rest lowercase before appending it to the result list. Finally, I join the titled words with a single space to form the resulting string, which I return. This approach ensures that all edge cases are handled correctly, including empty strings or strings with only whitespace characters.
